steps.title: Use the duration and cause clues
steps.content: The passage says mass extinctions unfold over thousands or millions of years and that multiple factors likely drove widespread species loss. Those two details directly support a statement about both timing and cause.

steps.title: Reject unsupported additions
steps.content: Choice B invents the idea that the species-loss number is underestimated. Choice C introduces fossil or geological detection, which the passage never discusses. Choice D invents recent discovery and poor understanding. The answer is A.
